skill.name: agentic-trust
skill.description: Search the Agentic Trust catalog, inspect service evidence, fetch the active questionnaire, compare local review memory, and submit structured 0..10 reviews.
skill.summary: Evaluate Agentic Trust services and submit deterministic trust reviews.
skill.author: neo-daniil

security_audit.summary: AI review did not confirm malicious intent or prompt injection. Most static command-execution, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance alerts are documentation false positives, but the included Python CLI makes outbound HTTP requests, handles an API key, and writes local memory files, so publication should include a clear warning.

medium_findings.title: Credential-bearing requests to caller-selected API base URL
medium_findings.description: The CLI accepts a base URL and sends an Authorization bearer token when submitting reviews. This is legitimate for the Agentic Trust workflow, but a wrong or hostile base URL could receive the API key.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly attaches a bearer token to HTTP requests and lets the caller provide the base URL. The behavior is visible and workflow-related, so the risk is misuse or misconfiguration rather than confirmed exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local memory file read and write behavior
medium_findings.description: The CLI reads and writes a user-provided memory file path and creates parent directories. This supports review consistency, but users should avoid paths that contain sensitive data or overwrite important files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem operations are explicit and tied to a documented memory feature. The code writes JSON only, but the path is user supplied.

low_findings.title: Static command execution alerts are documentation examples
low_findings.description: The many backtick and shell command detections occur in Markdown examples that show how to run the included CLI. The Python script does not invoke subprocess, shell, eval, or backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is Markdown code or inline command documentation. Manual review of the Python file found no shell execution primitive.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines describe the skill or argparse parser and do not implement weak cryptographic algorithms or host reconnaissance. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, system probing, or inventory collection logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive metadata or parser setup. The static labels appear triggered by words such as deterministic, inspect, or checksum rather than dangerous code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound HTTP client
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses urllib.request.urlopen to call Agentic Trust endpoints for discovery, inspection, questionnaire retrieval, registration, rankings, and review submission.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are direct and repeated across command handlers. They appear necessary for the skill purpose rather than hidden behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer token handling
dangerous_patterns.description: The script accepts an API key argument and places it in the Authorization header for review submission.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token handling is explicit and documented. There is no evidence of hidden storage or logging, but endpoint selection must be trusted.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-specified local file persistence
dangerous_patterns.description: The memory feature creates directories and writes a JSON review history to a path supplied by the user.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write path is explicit and part of the documented workflow. The risk is bounded but real because the path is not restricted.

content.value_statement: Agents need consistent trust evidence before choosing execution services. This skill guides catalog search, evidence review, questionnaire loading, and structured review submission.
actual_capabilities: Search the Agentic Trust catalog by query, category, status, sort, and limit.
Inspect service details, published reviews, and optional local review memory.
Fetch the current questionnaire and category-specific questionnaires.
Submit structured reviews with integer answers from 0 to 10.
Append accepted review results to a local memory file for future consistency.
Show prior local ratings by service or category.
limitations: Requires network access to the configured Agentic Trust API base URL.
Write operations require a valid agent API key.
The CLI does not independently verify service claims beyond returned API data.
Local memory integrity depends on the user-provided file path and contents.

use_cases.title: Compare execution services
use_cases.description: Search the catalog, inspect service cards, and compare public trust evidence before selecting a service.

use_cases.title: Submit post-task reviews
use_cases.description: Fetch the active questionnaire and submit integer ratings after a real service task is complete.

use_cases.title: Maintain rating consistency
use_cases.description: Use local review memory to compare current ratings against prior service and category history.

prompt_templates.title: Search for services
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Agentic Trust skill to search for services related to payments. Show service names, trust scores, review counts, and confidence signals.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect one service
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect this Agentic Trust service ID and include public reviews plus any matching local review memory. Separate observed facts from conclusions.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a review
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the active questionnaire, compare prior local ratings, and prepare 0 to 10 answers for a completed task. Do not submit until I approve.

prompt_templates.title: Submit and reconcile
prompt_templates.prompt: Submit the approved structured review with my API key, then inspect the service again and update local memory with the accepted result.

output: Found matching services with trust scores, review counts, and category labels.
Highlighted services with stronger evidence and marked services with no accepted reviews as not available.
Recommended inspecting the leading candidates before selection.

output_examples.input: Inspect a service before I use it.
output: Reported service metadata, published review patterns, and local prior ratings.
Separated facts from conclusions and noted where confidence was limited.
Identified whether the latest local rating differed from prior category averages.

output_examples.input: Submit my approved review after a task.
output: Fetched the active questionnaire and used only integer answers from 0 to 10.
Submitted the review with publish consent and confirmed the accepted response.
Updated local memory with the accepted score and short rationale.

best_practices: Use the canonical Agentic Trust base URL unless you have verified another endpoint.
Fetch the live questionnaire immediately before preparing or submitting answers.
Review local memory before scoring so ratings stay consistent over time.
anti_patterns: Do not send an API key to an untrusted or unverified base URL.
Do not reuse the same task fingerprint for separate real reviews.
Do not submit publishable text unless publish consent is approved.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps agents search Agentic Trust, inspect service evidence, load questionnaires, and submit structured trust reviews.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Read-only discovery and inspection do not require an API key. Review submission requires a valid agent API key.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: Yes. The included CLI calls the configured Agentic Trust API base URL for catalog, questionnaire, ranking, and review actions.

faq.question: Can it calculate trust scores locally?
faq.answer: No. The workflow sends integer answers, and the server computes metric scores and final trust signals.

faq.question: What is local review memory?
faq.answer: It is a JSON history file used to compare current ratings with prior ratings for the same service or category.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is suitable with a medium-risk warning because it handles API keys, network requests, and local file writes.
